notes.author: Dr. Daniel Okafor / Dr. Marisol Vega
notes.preview: Readmission for recurrent/progressed bilateral PE with RV strain and troponin elevation, after apixaban had been held 3 days for a planned biopsy. On heparin infusion.

notes.author: Dr. Olivia Reyes (Emergency Medicine)
notes.preview: Presented to the scheduled outpatient liver biopsy with recurrent dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain after apixaban had been held. Tachycardic 115, SpO2 93% on 4L. CTA: bilateral PE (progressed). New troponin elevation; TTE with RV strain. Heparin infusion started; biopsy cancelled.

notes.type: Interventional Radiology — Pre-Procedure / Biopsy Planning Note
notes.author: Dr. Jeff Weinstein (Interventional Radiology)
notes.preview: Pre-procedure planning for the outpatient CT-guided liver biopsy scheduled 8/10. Per IR protocol, instructs the patient to hold apixaban for 3 days prior; resume after hemostasis confirmed.

notes.author: Dr. Allyson Pishko (Hematology)
notes.preview: Interim outpatient visit ~2.5 weeks after discharge. Doing well on apixaban; symptoms resolved; no bleeding. Liver biopsy upcoming.

notes.author: Dr. Daniel Okafor / Dr. Marisol Vega
notes.preview: Discharge after acute PE/DVT, transitioned from heparin to apixaban. Liver mass concerning for malignancy — outpatient interventional-radiology biopsy and hepatology follow-up arranged. Return precautions for recurrent VTE symptoms.

notes.author: Dr. Daniel Okafor / Dr. Marisol Vega
notes.preview: HD5. Clinically improved, off oxygen. Triple-phase CT non-diagnostic but concerning. Plan to transition from heparin to apixaban and arrange the liver biopsy as an outpatient.

notes.author: Dr. Daniel Okafor / Dr. Marisol Vega
notes.preview: HD3. Liver MRI back: 17 cm right-lobe mass + left-lobe foci + right portal vein thrombus, high suspicion for malignancy, biopsy recommended. Symptoms improving on anticoagulation. Multidisciplinary discussion (heme, IR, hepatology) regarding biopsy timing.

notes.author: Dr. Allyson Pishko (Hematology)
notes.preview: Consult for management of unprovoked PE + extensive DVT in a previously healthy 43-year-old, with a concurrent liver mass concerning for malignancy.

notes.author: Dr. Olivia Reyes (ED) / Dr. Daniel Okafor (Medicine)
notes.preview: 43M previously healthy with 6 hours of pleuritic left chest pain and dyspnea. CTPA: segmental PE + incidental heterogeneous liver enhancement. RLE duplex: extensive DVT. Troponin negative, TTE no RV strain (low-risk PE). Essentially unprovoked. Anticoagulation started; admitted.

notes.author: Dr. Anita Joshi
notes.preview: Routine work physical ~10 months before presentation. Healthy; overweight (BMI ~29); normal labs. No prior clots, no anticoagulation, no liver disease. Establishes a healthy baseline.

_status: 10 notes (1 historical + 9 admission/transition/readmission). The IR 'hold apixaban 3 days' instruction lives only in NOTE007 (dated 8/8, gated to TP2) so it cannot hand away the TP1 DOAC-hold question. Management-decision notes (NOTE002, NOTE004, NOTE009) are deliberately sparse on the A&P.
